faqs.q: Is gambling disorder a real addiction?
faqs.a: Yes. It's recognized as a behavioral addiction, meaning it shares features with substance use disorders even though no substance is involved. Research suggests gambling can activate some of the same brain reward pathways.

faqs.q: What are signs of gambling disorder?
faqs.a: Signs can include needing to bet larger amounts to feel the same excitement, restlessness when trying to cut down, repeated unsuccessful efforts to stop, chasing losses, and lying to hide the extent of gambling. The behavior continues despite mounting consequences.

Yes. Effective treatments exist, including counseling and behavioral therapies, and support groups can help too. A return to gambling after stopping is best understood as part of a condition that can be managed.
